channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Sinner grinds down Zverev to successfully defend Wimbledon title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jannik Sinner successfully defended his Wimbledon title with a four-set victory against French Open champion Alexander Zverev on Centre Court.

item.title: Kim ends 1,001-day wait for win as Fitzpatrick, MacIntyre fall short in Scotland
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Tom Kim ended his 1,001-day wait for a fourth PGA Tour title by closing out an impressive two-shot win at the Genesis Scottish Open, as Robert MacIntyre missed out on a home victory.

item.title: Customs comptroller-general to receive UTA’s international humanitarian award
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Comptroller-General of the Nigeria Customs Service, Alhaji Bashir Adewale Adeniyi, has been named as winner of the prestigious International Humanitarian Award 2026 by the United Towns Agency for North-South Cooperation (UTA). The honour will be conferred on the Customs CG alongside other distin

item.title: Remember Lyrics by Rexxie Feat. Ayox
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Rexxie and Ayox Lyrics Remember by Rexxie and Ayox is a reflective, inspirational song centered on perseverance, gratitude, and personal growth, the lyrics look back on the artists' journey through hardship and financial struggles while appreciating how far they have come. Through heartfelt reflecti

item.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz indefinitely after warning shots at vessel
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards on Sunday announced the indefinite closure of the Strait of Hormuz after firing warning shots at a commercial vessel they accused of using an unauthorised shipping route, escalating tensions in one of the world’s most strategic maritime corridors. The announce

item.title: Iran declares Hormuz strait closed, US military insists traffic flowing
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was closed on Sunday while the United States insisted it remained open, after the confrontation over the vital waterway again sparked US and Iranian strikes. The post Iran declares Hormuz strait closed, US military insists traffic flowing appeared first on Vanguard New

item.title: Road projects, not failed water scheme, caused Abeokuta water disruptions – Ogun govt
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ogun State Water Corporation (OGSWC) has attributed the persistent disruption of pipe-borne water supply in parts of Abeokuta to damage inflicted on water distribution pipelines during road construction and urban renewal projects executed by the previous administration, dismissing claims that th

item.title: Ladipoe Champions African Creativity, Serenades Okonjo-Iweala At Geneva AI Summit
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian rapper Ladipoe has reflected on a week of high-level engagements in Geneva, where he joined global leaders in conversations on artificial intelligence, creativity and the future of intellectual property. The rapper participated in the AI for Good Summit and held meetings at the World Intell

item.title: World Cup latest: 2030 tournament could have 64 teams, says Infantino
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: England latest: We're 'knocking on the door’ of World Cup glory, says Kane
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Traders Grapple With World That’s Good for Dollar, Bad for Bonds
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: What’s good for the US dollar isn’t always good for US bonds — but investors are finding ways to work around it.

item.title: CNG Hails Rescue Of Oyo Schoolchildren, Demands Action To Free Abducted Borno Pupils
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Coalition of Northern Groups (CNG), has commended the successful rescue of the schoolchildren abducted in Oyo State, and demanded swift action to free abducted Borno pupils. The coalition also described the operation as proof that coordinated security efforts, effective intelligence gathering an

item.title: LASTMA digitally captures 38,000 traffic offenders in Q2 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Lagos State Traffic Management Authority (LASTMA) said it captured 38,000 vehicles for various traffic offences using its Traffic... The post LASTMA digitally captures 38,000 traffic offenders in Q2 2026 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Oyo Pupils, Teachers’ Rescue Rekindles Hope For Safer Nigeria – Oyintiloye
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A chieftain of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Osun State, Hon. Olatunbosun Oyintiloye has said the successful rescue of abducted pupils and teachers in Oyo State has boosted public confidence in President Bola Tinubu’s administration and strengthened prospects for economic growth and nationa

item.title: Hundreds evacuated from Paris suburb after weapons found near synagogue
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Three hundred people were evacuated on Saturday evening from a neighbourhood in Sarcelles, north of Paris, following reports of a suspicious vehicle, which was found to contain guns, including "a military-grade weapon", according interior minister Laurent Nunez. An investigation has been launched by

item.title: Niger Gov Inaugurates ICT Centre In Paiko Girls’ College
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Niger State Governor Umaru Bago has inaugurated a Digital Learning and ICT Centre at Martin Sanda Girls Science College, Paiko, facilitated by Justice Zaynab […] The post Niger Gov Inaugurates ICT Centre In Paiko Girls’ College appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Operation KOSAYE: Police charge 76 suspects to court for prostitution, robbery, others in Ogun
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ogun State Police Command says it has charged to court no fewer than 76 suspects arrested during its raid and bush-combing exercise codenamed Operation KOSAYE. DAILY POST had reported that the operation, meaning “No space” or “No chance” in English, is an initiative of th

item.title: CERON Slams N50,000 WAEC, NECO Registration Fee, Demands Reversal
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Campaign for Equal Rights and Opportunities for All Nigerians (CERON) has condemned the federal government’s approval of a N50,000 registration fee for the West African Examinations Council (WAEC) and the National Examinations Council (NECO), describing the decision as insensitive amid the count

item.title: Crackdown on restricted routes: Lagos confiscates 309 okadas
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: At the conclusion of the operation, 309 commercial motorcycles were impounded," Akerele said.

item.title: Atiku will defeat Tinubu in 2027, Imasuangbon predicts
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Chairman of Pacesetters Schools Abuja, Barrister Kenneth Imasuangbon, has predicted that former Vice President Atiku Abubakar will defeat President Bola Ahmed Tinubu in the 2027 presidential election to become Nigeria’s next President. Imasuangbon made the declaration on Sunday at the Graduation

item.title: Alleged Forgery: FG set to arraign ex-Minister Nnaji, alleged “fake” PFIPC DG Adeyemi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ikechukwu Nnochiri ABUJA— Barring any hitch, the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) will, on Monday, arraign the former Minister of Science and Technology, Chief Uche Nnaji, before the Federal High Court in Abuja. It would be recalled that security operativ

item.title: They can’t govern Nigeria – APC blasts opposition over missed INEC deadline
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The All Progressives Congress, APC, has criticized the failure of opposition political parties to meet the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, deadline for the submission of candidates for the 2027 general elections, saying it exposes their lack of preparedness to govern. The party made

item.title: Retiring England greats out cheaply as hosts set for heavy defeat at Lord's
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England are hurtling towards a seemingly inevitable defeat to India in the historic Women's Test at Lord's after closing on 130-6 in a record chase of 457, with Heather Knight and Tammy Beaumont out cheaply in their final knocks before international retirement.

item.title: Reps Begin Open National Assembly Tuesday
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives has announced the programme of activities for National Assembly Open Week 2026, themed “Three Years of the 10th Assembly: Advancing Transparency, Inclusion and Reform.” Spokesperson for the House, Hon. Akin Rotimi, who announced this in a statement on Sunday

item.title: Doctors demand health funding, digital medical training
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Adeola Badru The West African College of Physicians (WACP) has called on the Federal and state governments to prioritise sustainable healthcare financing, improve medical training infrastructure and adopt affordable digital technologies to address challenges confronting Nigeria’s health sector. T

item.title: Macron calls for 'constant vigilance' against antisemitism as France honors Alfred Dreyfus
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Sunday inaugurated a statue honoring Captain Alfred Dreyfus, whose wrongful 19th century treason conviction exposed deeply rooted anti-Jewish bias in France. Macron at the event decried a resurgence of the ″demons of antisemitism″ that have darkened France’s past

item.title: FG Nabs 2 Suspected Illegal Miners, Seals Mining Site In Osun
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has arrested two suspected illegal miners during an enforcement operation at an illegal mining site in Osun State. The operation was part of its intensified crackdown on illegal mining activities in the country. According to a statement on Sunday by Lara Owoeye-Wise, special a

item.title: Claude Code sends 33k tokens before reading the prompt; OpenCode sends 7k
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: This started based off of a hunch. We usually use OpenCode, but were 'forced' to use Claude Code for a while due to issues with Meridian. In that time, we saw the usage meter rise much, much more quickly than when using OpenCode.This was the initial anecdotal evidence, but we undertook this small st

item.title: Tinubu Celebrates Wole Soyinka At 92
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has joined other eminent Nigerians to congratulate Nobel laureate, Prof. Wole Soyinka, as he marks his 92nd birthday on Sunday, July 13. President Tinubu, in a statement issued by his spokesperson, described Soyink as one of Nigeria’s “Living repositories of history, knowledge,

item.title: Thousands gather to bid farewell to Qatar’s Father Emir Sheikh Hamad
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Thousands gathered in Doha to bid farewell to Qatar's Father Emir, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani.

item.title: 2027: ADC youths warn against poll rigging in South-South, vow mass mobilisation
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Davies Iheamnachor, Port Harcourt Youth leaders of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in the South-South geopolitical zone have warned against any attempt to manipulate the 2027 elections in the region, insisting that the polls must be free, fair and credible. The youths also pledged to mobili

item.title: LASTMA nabs 38,000 traffic offenders in three months
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “LASTMA is trying to move from physical enforcement to technology-driven enforcement, and that is why we captured more vehicles with technology in the second quarter of the year,” he said.

item.title: Woman dies shortly after alighting from commercial bus in Calabar
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ike Uchechukwu, Calabar A middle-aged woman, Mrs Affiong Peters, has died after she reportedly slumped shortly after alighting from a commercial bus in Calabar, Cross River State. The incident occurred at about 11:30pm on Saturday at Edgerly, along William George Street, in the state capital. The

item.title: APC Sweeps Edo LG election
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Edo State Independent Electoral Commission (EDSIEC) has declared the All Progressives Congress (APC) the winner of the local government election conducted across the state’s 18 local government areas and 192 wards. According to a report by The Nation, a total of 12 political parties fielde

Seyi Makinde, for the successful rescue of the pupils and teachers abducted in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, describing the development as a significant milestone in

item.title: 'We have another level we can reach' - Kane defends frustrated Tuchel
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Captain Harry Kane says England "have another level we can reach" as they bid to win the World Cup for the first time since 1966.

item.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni The Lagos State Environmental and Special Offences (Enforcement) Unit, popularly known as the Taskforce, has impounded 309 commercial motorcycles operating on restricted routes during a major enforcement exercise along the ECOWAS Road corridor and adjoining areas. The operation

item.description: The Christian Association of Nigeria, CAN, expresses its heartfelt gratitude to God Almighty following the successful rescue of the pupils, teachers, and other individuals abducted from schools in Oriire Local Government Area after enduring 56 challenging days in captivity. “Today is a day of

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Yoruba Council of Elders (YCE), has lauded President Bola Tinubu, Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde, and security agencies for the successful rescue of victims abducted in the recent attack in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. The Council described the rescue as most welcomed, saying it b

item.title: Stop linking banditry to Islam, Oluwo tells Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Oluwo of Iwoland, Oba Abdulrosheed Adewale Akanbi, has appealed to Nigerians to desist from linking the activities of bandits and other criminal elements to Islam, describing such narratives as unfair and misleading. The monarch said bandits have no religion and should be seen strictly as crimin

item.title: Kano State Pilgrims Welfare Board Commences 2027 Hajj Registration
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Kano State Pilgrims Welfare Board has officially commenced registration for the 2027 Hajj pilgrimage and announced an initial deposit of Eight Million Naira (₦8,000,000) for all intending pilgrims from the state. The announcement was made by the Director General of the Board, Alhaji Abubakar Ibr

item.title: BREAKING: Och’Otukpo orders herders to leave Otukpo Kingdom after deadly attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Och’Otukpo Odu, Chief John Eimonye, has directed all herders to vacate every part of Otukpo Kingdom with immediate effect. This follows Sunday’s attack on Nobi Village in Otukpo District where several persons were killed. The directive was issued after an attack allegedly carried out

item.title: Barau defends state police, says initiative will tackle insecurity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The deputy president of the Senate, Barau I. Jibrin, has described President Bola Tinubu's bid to establish state police as a masterstroke that will strengthen Nigeria's security architecture and tackle rising insecurity across the country. The post Barau defends state police, says initiative will t

item.title: Portable reveals how selling pure water helped him through childhood hardship
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigerian singer Habeeb Okikiola, popularly known as Portable, has shared how losing his mother at the age of 13 changed the course of his life.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: They are immensely talented, creative and ingenious minds breaking academic boundaries to set global records. From humble backgrounds, they defy read more Making the best use of young Nigerian geniuses

item.title: Five Yobe residents hospitalised after rabies-infected dog attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Five residents of Malam Matari community, on the outskirts of Damaturu, are receiving medical treatment after being bitten by a dog confirmed to have rabies, in an incident that has raised fresh concerns over public health and animal disease control in Yobe State. The attack also affected livestock,

item.title: Oriire mass kidnap, forty-four lives, politics, and ignorance of the commentari
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: I have watched, with a mixture of pity and disgust, as people who have never sat in a situation room, never studied a hostage network, never lost a night's sleep over a tactical map, stand on the sidelines and hand out verdicts.

item.title: Nigerian Exchange Engages FTSE Russell, Investors Over Nigeria’s T+1 Settlement, Market Reclassification
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Exchange Group has engaged with FTSE Russell, global institutional investors and custodians in London to address concerns following the index provider’s decision to pause Nigeria’s reclassification to Frontier Market status. The engagements centered on Nigeria’s new T+1 settlement cycle

item.title: Human trafficking: Makinde to reintegrate migrant returnees
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The governor said the initiative aims to restore dignity to returnees, create sustainable livelihoods, and address the socio-economic factors driving irregular migration and human trafficking. item.description: The Ogun State Police Command on Sunday disclosed the arrest of 43 suspects after a coordinated raid on criminal hideouts as part of the ongoing “Operation Kosaye.” The joint operation was launched last month by the Ogun and Lagos commands in a bid to rid the two states of banditry, kidn

item.description: Super Eagles goalkeeper Stanley Nwabali has completed a return to South African side Chippa United, five months after parting ways with the club. The 30-year-old is back with the Chilli Boys ahead of the 2026/27 campaign following a spell away from competitive football after his departure in Februar

item.title: Are tanks aging like the cavalry in WW1?
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
The rapid pace of technological advances in drone warfare, guided by artificial intelligence in Ukraine and elsewhere in the world, has fundamentally changed the face of conflict. There is a question about how radical that change is: an evolution or a revolution in warfare?
And it's causing all so

item.title: Bloomberg This Weekend | Sen. Lindsey Graham Dead, CENTCOM Launches Strikes on Iran
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description:
The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York.
Joined by Adm. Daryl Caudle, US Dept of War Chief of Naval Operations, Kurt Volker, fmr. US Ambassador to N

item.dc:creator: The Whistler

Justice Peter Kekemeke of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court, Abuja, had ruled against

item.title: UNESCO REF, UNICAF partner to offer Nigerians 86% scholarship, complement NELFund
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Read and Earn Federation for UNESCO (UNESCO REF) and UNICAF have launched a strategic partnership aimed at expanding access to globally recognised higher education for Nigerians through an 86 per cent tuition scholarship scheme that complements the Federal Government's Nigeria Education Loan Fun

item.title: World Cup 2026: I’ve Played Against Everyone Except England –Messi
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Argentina star Lionel Messi has disclosed that England is the only major football powerhouse he has never played against in his entire career. The Argentine star expressed excitement about facing them in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final, calling the historic match-up a unique game. Read Also:Worl

item.title: Lagos task force impounds 309 motorcycles, clears Lagos-Badagry expressway
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Lagos State Task Force has impounded 309 commercial motorcycles during a major enforcement operation along the The post Lagos task force impounds 309 motorcycles, clears Lagos-Badagry expressway appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Stanton Warns Hormuz Closure Hurts Economy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Rep. Greg Stanton says the Trump administration needs a stronger diplomatic strategy to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and negotiate a more accountable agreement with Iran, warning the conflict has increased economic and geopolitical risks. He also defends closer ties with Canada, Mexico and other US a

item.title: Tinubu hails Soyinka as “national repository of history” on 92nd birthday
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Juliana Taiwo-Obalonye, Abuja President Bola Tinubu has paid tribute to Nobel laureate Wole Soyinka as the celebrated playwright turns 92, describing him as a “global icon” and “one of our nation’s living repositories of history, knowledge, and courage.” In a State House statement he personally

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Police to arraign Adeniyi Adeyemi Tuesday
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The police are to arraign Adeniyi Adeyemi, the Director-General of ‘the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council, PFIPC, in the Federal High Court on Tuesday. The police, in a fresh court document marked FHC/ABJ/CR/562/2025, presently charged Adeyemi and two others with eight counts

item.title: ADC senatorial candidate urges stronger security after deadly Otukpo attacks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The senatorial candidate decried the recurring attacks on communities across the senatorial district and urged security agencies to intensify efforts to protect lives and ensure those responsible were brought to justice. The post ADC senatorial candidate urges stronger security after deadly Otukpo a
